+/* NULL-safe variant of RedisModule_FreeString(). If 'str' is NULL,
+ * this macro is a no-op, similar to how free(NULL) behaves.
+ * Defined as a macro so that modules can use it even when loaded
+ * into older Redis versions that predate this addition. */
+#define RedisModule_FreeStringSafe(ctx, str) do { if (str) RedisModule_FreeString(ctx, str); } while(0)

discussion_comments.comment_body: Fixed in commit 343735f: the macro now uses a temporary variable `_rfs_str` to evaluate `str` exactly once.

REDISMODULE_API int (*RedisModule_SetKeyMeta)(RedisModuleKeyMetaClassId id, RedisModuleKey *key, uint64_t metadata) REDISMODULE_ATTR;
 REDISMODULE_API int (*RedisModule_GetKeyMeta)(RedisModuleKeyMetaClassId id, RedisModuleKey *key, uint64_t *metadata) REDISMODULE_ATTR;
 
+/* RedisModule_FreeString() is defined here as a NULL-safe macro so that
+ * modules compiled against this header are protected against accidental
+ * NULL frees (mirroring free(NULL) semantics), while retaining full ABI
+ * compatibility with older Redis builds:  the API dict entry
+ * "RedisModule_FreeString" still resolves to the raw function pointer
+ * (_RedisModule_FreeString), so modules built against older headers
+ * continue to call it directly without any issue.
+ *
+ * The temporary variable avoids double-evaluation of 'str' when 'str' is
+ * a side-effecting expression such as array[i++]. */
+#define RedisModule_FreeString(ctx, str) do {       \
+    void *_rfs_str = (str);                         \

discussion_comments.comment_body: Fixed in commit 486d088: changed `void *_rfs_str` to `RedisModuleString *_rfs_str`, restoring compile-time type checking and removing the need for the explicit cast.

return $retval

+# Ping server with a timeout. Returns 1 if server responds within timeout_ms,
+# otherwise returns 0. Uses blocking TCP connect (instant on localhost) and
+# Tcl's event loop (fileevent + vwait + after) for reliable timeouts.
+# For TLS, the handshake is performed separately in non-blocking mode so that
+# a paused/unresponsive server triggers the timeout instead of hanging.
+proc ping_server_with_timeout {host port timeout_ms} {
+    set retval 0
+    set fd {}
+    set wait_var "::ping_wait_[incr ::ping_server_uid]"
+    if {[catch {
+        # TCP connect is always blocking: instant on localhost even if the
+        # server process is paused, because the kernel handles SYN/ACK.
+        set fd [socket $host $port]
+
+        if {$::tls} {
+            # Perform TLS handshake in non-blocking mode with a timeout.
+            # We avoid ::tls::socket because it blocks indefinitely when the
+            # server is paused (SIGSTOP) -- the TLS handshake requires active
+            # server participation that a paused process cannot provide.
+            ::tls::import $fd \
+                -cafile "$::tlsdir/ca.crt" \
+                -certfile "$::tlsdir/client.crt" \
+                -keyfile "$::tlsdir/client.key"
+            fconfigure $fd -blocking 0
+            set hs_done 0
+            set hs_end [expr {[clock milliseconds] + $timeout_ms}]
+            while {!$hs_done && [clock milliseconds] < $hs_end} {
+                if {[catch {set hs_done [::tls::handshake $fd]}]} break
+                if {!$hs_done} {
+                    set $wait_var ""
+                    after 10 [list set $wait_var "x"]
+                    vwait $wait_var
+                }
+            }
+            if {!$hs_done} {
+                error "TLS handshake did not complete"
+            }
+            fconfigure $fd -blocking 1
+        }
+
+        fconfigure $fd -translation binary -buffering full
+        puts $fd "PING\r
"
+        flush $fd
+
+        # Read timeout via event loop: whichever fires first unblocks vwait
+        set $wait_var ""
+        set timer [after $timeout_ms [list set $wait_var "timeout"]]
+        fileevent $fd readable [list set $wait_var "readable"]
+        vwait $wait_var
+
+        after cancel $timer
+        fileevent $fd readable {}
+
+        if {[set $wait_var] eq "readable"} {
+            set reply [gets $fd]
+            if {[string range $reply 0 0] eq {+} ||
+                [string range $reply 0 0] eq {-}} {
+                set retval 1
+            }
+        }
+        close $fd
+        set fd {}
+    } e]} {
+        catch {if {$fd ne {}} {close $fd}}
